kpis.Definition: Total individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing year-to-date across all HC core initiatives.
Average number of days between a person's first contact with the homeless response system and their placement in stable housing.
Percentage of people who return to homelessness within 12 months of being housed.
Cumulative count of affordable housing units produced, preserved, or protected from loss through HC-supported partnerships.
Number of organizations actively participating in HC-led convenings, coalitions, or collective impact initiatives during the reporting period.
Number of new formal partnerships established with organizations led by Black, Indigenous, and People of Color during the plan period.
Number of policy changes, funding allocations, or legislative outcomes directly attributable to HC's advocacy and research efforts.
Average rating (1–5 scale) from the annual partner survey measuring HC's value as a convening and coordinating body.
Percentage of staff employed at the start of the year who remain employed at year-end. Target: 85% or above.
Percentage of total revenue from the largest single funding source. Lower concentration = healthier diversification. Target: no source exceeds 40%.
Number of months of operating expenses held in unrestricted cash reserves. Target: minimum 3 months.
Percentage of grants and contracts meeting all funder reporting, programmatic, and financial compliance requirements by their deadlines.
Average score (1–5 scale) from the bi-annual staff engagement and workplace satisfaction survey.
Average number of professional development and training hours completed per staff member annually. Target: minimum 20 hours.
Staff + board completion
Percentage of HC decision-making bodies (board, committees, advisory groups) that include meaningful representation from People with Lived Experience of homelessness or housing instability.
Percentage of staff who identify as Black, Indigenous, or People of Color. Tracked across all levels including leadership.
Percentage of HC's formal community partnerships that are with BIPOC-led organizations, measured against a long-term target.
Percentage of HC vendor contracts and service agreements awarded through a process that explicitly applies DEIB criteria.
Survey-based rating of trust and confidence in HC among BIPOC community members in HC's service regions.

placements.Definition: Monthly count of individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing through HC-supported programs. Target is set quarterly based on capacity and seasonal trends.
Monthly count of individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing through HC-supported programs. Target is set quarterly based on capacity and seasonal trends.
Monthly count of individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing through HC-supported programs. Target is set quarterly based on capacity and seasonal trends.
Monthly count of individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing through HC-supported programs. Target is set quarterly based on capacity and seasonal trends.
Monthly count of individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing through HC-supported programs. Target is set quarterly based on capacity and seasonal trends.
Monthly count of individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing through HC-supported programs. Target is set quarterly based on capacity and seasonal trends.
Monthly count of individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing through HC-supported programs. Target is set quarterly based on capacity and seasonal trends.
Monthly count of individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing through HC-supported programs. Target is set quarterly based on capacity and seasonal trends.
Monthly count of individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing through HC-supported programs. Target is set quarterly based on capacity and seasonal trends.
Monthly count of individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing through HC-supported programs. Target is set quarterly based on capacity and seasonal trends.
Monthly count of individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing through HC-supported programs. Target is set quarterly based on capacity and seasonal trends.
Monthly count of individuals and families successfully placed in stable housing through HC-supported programs. Target is set quarterly based on capacity and seasonal trends.
Sum of all monthly placements and targets for the full reporting period.

partners.Definition: 501(c)(3) organizations including homeless service providers, housing developers, legal aid, health, and social service agencies engaged in HC-led convenings or initiatives.
Municipal, state, and federal government agencies and departments participating in HC collective impact work, including housing authorities and CoC leads.
Private sector businesses, real estate developers, financial institutions, and employers engaged as partners in HC's housing affordability and workforce initiatives.
Private and community foundations providing funding and strategic partnership support to HC and its regional initiatives.

deib.Definition: Percentage of all staff and board members who have completed the required annual DEIB training curriculum in the current plan year. Tracks both completion rate and training quality via post-survey.

Percentage of formal HC decision-making bodies — including the Board of Directors, advisory committees, and initiative steering groups — that include at least one Person with Lived Experience of homelessness or housing instability in a meaningful, compensated role.
Percentage of HC staff who identify as Black, Indigenous, or People of Color (BIPOC), tracked across all levels. Leadership-level representation is tracked separately to identify pipeline gaps.
Percentage of HC's formal community partnerships that are with organizations founded and led by BIPOC individuals, measured against the plan period target of 70%.
Percentage of HC vendor, contractor, and consultant agreements awarded through a procurement process that explicitly applies DEIB criteria including minority-owned business preferences.
Composite survey score measuring the level of trust, confidence, and sense of inclusion that BIPOC community members in HC's service regions feel toward The Housing Collective. Administered annually.
Measures whether housing placement rates for BIPOC individuals are proportional to their representation in the overall homeless population. A score below 100% indicates a disparity gap.
Percentage of identified inequities from the most recent internal equity audit that have been addressed or remediated. Audits cover hiring, compensation, procurement, and program access.

orgHealth.Definition: Percentage of staff employed at the start of the fiscal year who remain employed at year-end. Calculated as: (staff at year-end ÷ staff at year-start) × 100. Target: 85% or above.
Percentage of total annual revenue derived from the single largest funding source. A lower percentage indicates healthier diversification. Target: no single source exceeds 40% of total revenue.
Number of months of average operating expenses held in unrestricted cash or liquid reserves. Calculated as: (unrestricted reserves ÷ average monthly expenses). Target: minimum 3 months.
Percentage of all active grants and contracts where HC has met all funder reporting, programmatic milestone, and financial compliance requirements by their stated deadlines. Target: 99% or above.
Average score (1–5 scale) from HC's bi-annual all-staff engagement and workplace satisfaction survey. Covers dimensions including culture, leadership, compensation, and professional growth.

Average number of formal professional development, training, and learning hours completed per full-time staff member annually. Includes internal training, external conferences, and coursework. Target: 20+ hours.

Count of formally documented collaborations, shared resources, joint activities, or warm referrals between HC's three core initiatives (Opening Doors, Centers for Housing Opportunity, Innovation Lab) in the reporting period.
Average number of calendar days from the date a job posting is published to the date an offer is accepted. Measures recruitment efficiency. Target: 30 days or fewer.
